summary: Descriptive overview of breachstandard in substantive law: reasonable person, custom, negligence per se.
key_points: reasonable person is a fundamental concept in tort law.
Legal standards for breachstandard are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine breachstandard in litigation.
The doctrine of breachstandard has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding breachstandard requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
